setting.note: Woolf names the Isle of Skye, but the house, the bay and the lighthouse are drawn entirely from St Ives, Cornwall, where the Stephen family spent childhood summers. The map runs from Talland House, the Woolfs' real holiday home above the town, out across St Ives Bay to Godrevy Lighthouse, the light the children could see from the garden and the one the family in the novel longs to reach.

rights.originalWork: Public domain (Virginia Woolf died 1941). Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.attribution: To the Lighthouse, Virginia Woolf (1927). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or estate.

palette.note: The sea-blue of St Ives Bay, the water between the garden and the Godrevy light. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
